name: Texas Beef Chili
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Beef chuck or shin, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
1 lb Spanish chorizo sausage, sliced into 1/4 inch thick pieces
4 lbs Canned Italian plum tomatoes, drained and chopped
24 oz Beer, Dos Equis or other
recipeInstructions:
1. Toss the meat with 3 tbsp olive oil and 2 tbsp chili powder in a non-aluminum bowl. Let stand in refrigerator overnight.
2. Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a large, heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the meat in batches (do not crowd) on all sides, about 5 minutes.
3. Transfer the meat to a large pot using a slotted spoon. Add chorizo and brown well. Transfer to the pot using a slotted spoon.
4. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add more oil to skillet if necessary. Add onions and cook until translucent, about 10 minutes.
5. Add garlic, chili powder, oregano, cumin, salt, and pepper. Stir for 3 minutes then transfer to the pot.
6. Stir in tomatoes, beer, and tomato paste. Bring to a boil then reduce heat.
7. Cover and simmer until meat is very tender, stirring occasionally, about 3 hours. Uncover during last hour if necessary to thicken the liquid into sauce.
8. Good accompaniments include pinto beans and salsa as side dishes. Serves 6 generously.
